channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

Bin needs to be emptied.

item.description:
Bin needs to be emptied.

item.title: Drug dealing drug use, 11th July
item.description:
Since the women living in the hmo skinny white lady has been living in here on the corner their has been a tent on the cemetry and now on the embankment, the hostel near the catholic church also socialise with these on the bench and they are using the stairs at the bowling alley to underground carpark to use drugs and go a toilet
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Methuen Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree branches, 11th July
item.description:
The overgrown tree branch is obstructing the pavement and making it unsafe, especially for wheelchair users. This is an accessibility and safety hazard. Please arrange urgent maintenance to clear the obstruction.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 61, Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped sofa and table, 11th July
item.description:
Sofa and table dumped on the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stoke Newington High Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road and paths not being cleaned, 11th July
item.description:
Reported many times that the road is not being cleaned. Previous reports closed down but still no action to clean the paths and road and drains.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15;15a, London Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown grass in public land accessed via Avondale Ave, Burlington Rise and path adjoining St Mary's Church, 11th July
item.description:
The grass has been left to grow since last year. It’s now so long that it prevents dog owners from locating and safely picking up waste. Also, the overgrown vegetation has created several severe health and safety hazards for local residents and pets: • Hidden Rubbish: We have found discarded gin/beer bottles, cannisters, cigarettes, burnt patches where someone has lit a fire. Because the grass is overgrown, this broken glass is entirely hidden, creating a severe laceration risk for children, walkers, and pets, and the overgrown grass represents a fire risk. • Pet Injuries: Mature grass seeds are embedding themselves into dogs' paws and ears, causing painful infections and costly vet visits. • Parasite Risks: The long grass provides an ideal breeding ground for ticks, significantly increasing the risk of Lyme disease for both pets and walking residents. • Severe Allergies: The unmanaged grass generates high volumes of pollen. This pollen blows directly into nearby homes and clings to dogs' coats, severely aggravating respiratory issues and allergies for local families. • Biological Hazard: Hidden dog faeces pose a serious risk of Toxocariasis, particularly to young children using the green space. Please arrange for this area to be mown urgently, and ensure in future the grass is cut regularly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Avondale Avenue, Brunswick Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 10th July
item.description:
Sofa has been here for days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Holmbury View, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Small sinkhole on middle of the pavement, 10th July
item.description:
This sinkhole has appeared in the pavement on the path between Montagu Road and Barrowfield Close N9 can it be investigated before someone falls through it and it gets bigger as it is on a well used pathway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Montagu Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
The street plate for Crosslet Street is missing

item.title: Posters and advertisements being attached to road railings, 10th July
item.description:
Banners/ advertisements being flyposted
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Square, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Communal block rubbish bins being left on pavement, 10th July
item.description:
The communal waste bins are being left on the pavement and not put out and taken back by the cleaning/collection team, and they are partially blocking the pavement and encouraging people who aren't residents to dump rubbish here. This has been going on for about a month, and was never an issue before.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Townsend Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: As you look left from Cherry Tree Walk into Eden Park Avenue the first tree has lower foliage which requires cutting back as it is obstructing vision of oncoming traffic, 10th July
item.description:
Recurring problem

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 114, Eden Park Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree support broken, 10th July
item.description:
The support for the tree has come out of the ground and now the tree has fallen to an angle. Needs propping up again to correct this

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mainridge Road, Chislehurst
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: We need a bin, 10th July
item.description:
There is no bin anywhere within the development. As a result people leave trash on the ground or on benches. It's also difficult for pet owners who want to pick up after their dogs and dispose. The area is heavily walked by pedestrians, especially since a shop opened on the ground floor. The nearest bin is at least 5 minutes walk away.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rossett Way, Deptford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of a freezer, 10th July
item.description:
2 houses down keep fly tipping, tonight (10/07/26) they have taken a freezer and dumped it by the recycling bins in between Tappesfield Road and Nunhead Grove. This happened at 10:03pm (between the flats)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Daniels Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 10th July
item.description:
This has been here for weeks. Last time the fly tipping was cleared but the commercial waste bin was left which led to new fly tipping. This bin needs to go. Now someone has tipped it over. Please solve

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tabernacle Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet
